steps.title: Use the lack-of-data clue
steps.content: Before Mariner 2, Earth-based observations had yielded very little information about Venus because its atmosphere is so thick. Astronomers' ideas therefore would have been speculative rather than firmly supported by direct evidence.

steps.title: Reject evidence-based words
steps.content: Choice A suggests summaries of known information. Choice C means examples that illustrate a principle. Choice D means findings drawn from evidence. The needed word means educated guesses formed when firm evidence is lacking. The answer is B.
